surface.name: SUSE Documentation, with the Rancher Prime Helm CLI proof of concept as a blocked candidate
surface.selection_basis: SUSE's documentation home presents many peer product families without naming a company-wide developer starting point. The Rancher Prime Helm CLI Quick Start is retained as a bounded candidate surface because it explicitly promises a proof-of-concept installation, but its required chart repository URL is hidden behind SUSE Customer Center authentication.

alternatives_considered.name: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer operating-system product with installation and administration journeys rather than the declared company-wide developer default.

alternatives_considered.name: K3s
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer cloud-native product and is also used as an implementation prerequisite inside the selected Rancher candidate.

alternatives_considered.name: Deploy Rancher on Amazon EKS
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Rancher deployment index presents EKS, Equinix Metal, manual Helm, and AWS SaaS as alternative routes without a universal provider choice.

entry_point.developer_intent: Choose a SUSE developer product and reach the first terminal state that its official quick-start documentation names.
assumed_prior_state: No SUSE product choice, Rancher installation, Kubernetes cluster, kubeconfig, Helm repository, cert-manager installation, domain, or Rancher administrator password is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established from a no-account state by public official documentation.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended candidate terminal is a browser displaying the Rancher user interface, but the public path cannot obtain the required Rancher Prime Helm chart repository URL.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The downstream quick start says the Rancher UI should appear at the configured hostname; the public no-account path cannot reach that signal reproducibly.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Navigate to the configured sslip.io hostname and see the Rancher UI

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The quick start names the browser-visible Rancher UI as its proof-of-concept terminal, but it requires a Prime-only chart repository URL whose linked documentation redirects to SUSE identity authentication and does not expose account creation, entitlement, authorization, or the URL publicly.

prerequisites.requirement: Provide a Linux virtual machine reachable from the workstation; the guide says not to install on localhost and requires saving the machine's IP address.
Select a supported Kubernetes version and have kubectl and Helm installed on the local workstation.

An authenticated SUSE Customer Center session is required to open the Prime-only documentation and retrieve the Rancher Prime Helm chart repository URL; the public guide does not enumerate account creation, access approval, or the hidden URL.
Have root or equivalent sudo access on the Linux virtual machine and permission to copy its K3s kubeconfig to the workstation.
Allow the workstation and browser to reach the Linux node on the Kubernetes API and web-service ports used by the guide.
Choose either the guide's IP-based sslip.io hostname or a real domain that points to the Linux node.

primary_path.action: Open SUSE Documentation and choose the product family whose first mile is being measured.
details: The home lists peer operating-system, cloud-native, edge, storage, virtualization, security, and AI products.
primary_path.output: The product-specific documentation entry points are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: A product family can be selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: SUSE does not name one company-wide developer first mile on this page.

primary_path.action: For the retained candidate, choose SUSE Rancher Prime and open its deployment quick starts.
details: The deployment index offers Amazon EKS, Equinix Metal, manual installation, and Rancher for AWS SaaS.
primary_path.output: A provider-specific or manual deployment guide is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The Helm CLI Quick Start is open for the manual proof-of-concept candidate.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The product and deployment route change the prerequisites, commands, account gates, and terminal state.

primary_path.action: Follow the Prime-only documentation link to obtain the required Rancher Prime Helm chart repository URL.
details: The official public quick start states that authentication is required and directs the developer to use SUSE Customer Center credentials.
primary_path.input: SUSE Customer Center credentials and access to the Prime-only documentation
primary_path.output: A redirect to SUSE identity authentication is publicly observable.
primary_path.success_signal: No completed login, entitlement, chart URL, or return state is documented on the public official surface.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The required chart URL and every authenticated intermediate state remain hidden from public documentation.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The public Helm CLI Quick Start requires `<helm-chart-repo-url>` from Prime-only documentation, but that link redirects to SUSE identity authentication and does not document account creation, entitlement, authorization, the returned URL, or convergence back to the quick start.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The Rancher chart repository cannot be added and the documented cert-manager, Rancher install, hostname, and browser-verification sequence cannot be connected chronologically to a no-account starting state without inference.

branches.condition: A different SUSE product is intended
branches.path: Use that product family's own setup or quick-start documentation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the complete journey and terminal success rather than merely changing one step.

branches.condition: A managed or provider-specific Rancher route is preferred
branches.path: Choose Amazon EKS, Equinix Metal, or Rancher for AWS SaaS instead of the Helm CLI route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds provider-specific accounts, infrastructure, access, and commands.

friction_gates.description: SUSE exposes many peer product families without a company-wide default developer path.
Rancher itself offers multiple deployment quick starts with different provider and account requirements.
The required Rancher Prime chart repository URL is available only through Prime-only documentation that redirects to SUSE identity authentication.

sources.title: SUSE Documentation
evidence_supported: Absence of one company-wide starting route

sources.title: SUSE Rancher Prime Deployment Quick Start Guides
sections_used: SUSE Rancher Prime Deployment Quick Start Guides

sources.title: Helm CLI Quick Start
sections_used: Save Your Linux Node IP Address

uncertainties.question: Which SUSE product and deployment mode should define a company-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes the entire chronology, prerequisites, access gates, and success boundary.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation home exposes many peer products, and even the selected Rancher family exposes multiple peer deployment routes.

uncertainties.question: What exact public sequence creates or authorizes an SCC account, exposes the Rancher Prime Helm chart repository URL, and returns the developer to the installation?
uncertainties.impact: Without that bridge, the Helm repository command and every later proof-of-concept action are not reproducible from the declared no-account state.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The public quick start shows a placeholder and an authenticated link; the linked Prime-only documentation redirects to SUSE identity authentication.

excluded_after_success: Logging in, changing the bootstrap password, importing downstream clusters, deploying workloads, production certificates, backups, high availability, monitoring, and production hardening occur after the Rancher UI first appears.
